name: slop-scan
title: Slop Scan
purpose: Capability: /adv-slop-scan command — detect AI-generated code quality issues including defensive overkill, deep nesting, and other slop patterns

requirements.title: AST-First Detection Strategy
requirements.body: /adv-slop-scan must attempt AST-based analysis before falling back to regex or heuristic methods. One primary AST tool per language is used; fallback is a brace/indent counter annotated as degraded.

scenarios.title: TypeScript/JavaScript uses ESLint rules
given: ESLint is available in the project
then: ESLint max-depth rule is used for nesting detection
ESLint complexity rule is used for cyclomatic complexity
Results are mapped to MAINT-004 smell ID

scenarios.title: Python uses radon
then: radon cc is used for cyclomatic complexity
Results above complexity_threshold are mapped to MAINT-004

scenarios.title: Go uses gocyclo
then: gocyclo is used for cyclomatic complexity
Results above complexity_threshold are mapped to MAINT-004

scenarios.title: Fallback when no AST tool available
given: No AST tool is installed for the detected language
then: A brace/indent counter is used as fallback
All findings from fallback have detectionMethod: 'degraded'
Report includes [DEGRADED: AST tool unavailable] annotation

meta.supersedes: Legacy advance-meta slop-scan AST/degraded fallback coverage.

requirements.title: Configurable Detection Thresholds
requirements.body: Detection thresholds must have smart defaults and support per-project overrides via project.json features.slop_scan block. Defaults must not produce false positives for normal single-guard or single-catch patterns.

scenarios.title: Default thresholds applied when no config present
then: nesting_depth_threshold defaults to 4
defensive_guard_threshold defaults to 3
complexity_threshold defaults to 10
ast_timeout_ms defaults to 10000

scenarios.title: Partial overrides respected
then: nesting_depth_threshold is 6
All other thresholds remain at defaults

scenarios.title: AST tool timeout enforced
given: An AST tool takes longer than ast_timeout_ms to complete on a file
scenarios.when: Phase 1 detection runs on that file
then: The tool invocation is terminated
The file falls back to degraded detection
Report annotates the file with [DEGRADED: AST timeout]

meta.supersedes: Legacy advance-meta slop-scan threshold override coverage.

requirements.title: Defensive Overkill Detection
requirements.body: /adv-slop-scan must detect overly defensive code patterns: redundant null/undefined guard chains, paranoid pre-condition checks on the same value, and unreachable fallback branches. These are reported as QUAL-011.

scenarios.title: Redundant guard chain detected
given: A function checks the same variable for null, undefined, and falsy in separate consecutive conditions
then: A QUAL-011 finding is emitted
Severity is at least medium
confidence field reflects detection certainty

scenarios.title: Single guard clause is not flagged
given: A function has exactly one null check before using a value
then: No QUAL-011 finding is emitted for that function

meta.supersedes: Legacy advance-meta defensive-overkill and clean-code coverage.

requirements.title: Always-On Structured Output Fields
requirements.body: Every finding in JSON output must include nestingDepth, complexity, confidence, and detectionMethod fields regardless of verbosity flags. Text output must surface these fields inline.

scenarios.title: JSON findings always include diagnostic fields
given: A finding is produced by any detection method
then: The finding object includes nestingDepth (number or null)
The finding object includes complexity (number or null)
The finding object includes confidence ('high' | 'medium' | 'low')
The finding object includes detectionMethod ('ast' | 'regex' | 'heuristic' | 'degraded')

scenarios.title: Text output surfaces diagnostic fields inline
then: The finding block includes detectionMethod and confidence
nestingDepth and complexity are shown when non-null

meta.supersedes: Legacy advance-meta structured diagnostic field coverage.

requirements.title: Dead Code Tool Preference
requirements.body: knip must be the primary dead code analyzer for TypeScript/JavaScript projects. ts-prune is treated as a legacy fallback only and must be documented as such.

scenarios.title: knip used as primary dead code tool
scenarios.when: Dead code detection runs in Phase 1
then: knip is invoked as the primary tool
ts-prune is not invoked unless knip fails

scenarios.title: ts-prune used only as legacy fallback
scenarios.when: Dead code detection runs in Phase 1
then: ts-prune is invoked as fallback
Report annotates findings with [LEGACY TOOL: ts-prune]

requirements.title: False-Positive Confidence and Actionability Control
requirements.body: /adv-slop-scan must classify findings by confidence and actionability so known clean samples stay below the accepted false-positive threshold for actionable findings while true high-confidence slop remains reported.

scenarios.title: Known clean sample stays below actionable false-positive target
given: A named clean regression sample containing ordinary guard clauses, public API boundary validation, example snippets, and test fixtures
The accepted false-positive target is 10 percent for actionable findings
then: High-confidence and medium-confidence actionable false positives are less than or equal to 10 percent of actionable findings
Single guard clauses and single I/O try/catch blocks do not produce QUAL-011 findings
Clean code below the configured nesting threshold does not produce MAINT-004 findings

scenarios.title: Known dirty sample remains detected
given: A named dirty regression sample containing redundant guard chains and deep nesting at or above configured thresholds
then: A QUAL-011 finding is emitted for redundant defensive checks
A MAINT-004 finding is emitted for deep nesting
The findings remain actionable when confidence is high or medium and source evidence is present

scenarios.title: Phase 1 confidence defaults are deterministic
given: Phase 1 emits findings from AST, regex, or degraded fallback detection
then: AST-backed structural findings default to confidence: 'high'
Regex-only defensive-overkill findings default to confidence: 'medium' unless corroborated by same-identifier redundant guard evidence
Degraded fallback findings default to confidence: 'low' unless corroborated by another detector

requirements.title: Low-Confidence Finding Grouping
requirements.body: /adv-slop-scan must preserve low-confidence findings for auditability while grouping them separately from actionable findings in human-readable output.

scenarios.title: Text output separates low-confidence findings
then: The finding appears in a low-confidence or non-blocking section
The finding is separated from actionable high-confidence and medium-confidence findings
The report states that low-confidence findings are not blocking by default

scenarios.title: JSON output preserves low-confidence findings
then: The finding remains present in findings[]
The finding includes confidence, detectionMethod, nestingDepth, and complexity fields
The finding includes grouping or actionability metadata that marks it non-blocking

requirements.title: Context-Window Suppression
requirements.body: Phase 2 heuristic scanners must treat ADV context packets, examples, task summaries, and fixture descriptions as non-scannable context unless the issue is present in an actual target source file with source evidence.

scenarios.title: Prompt context is not a finding location
given: A Phase 2 scanner prompt includes ADV change context, task summaries, examples, or known fixture snippets
then: The scanner does not emit findings against prompt context text
Every finding cites a target source file and line or scoped source evidence
Context packet text is used only for orientation

scenarios.title: Examples and fixtures are not treated as product defects by default
given: A scanned file is an example, fixture, or test sample intentionally demonstrating a smell
then: The scanner either suppresses the finding or marks it low-confidence/non-blocking unless the file is within the explicit target product scope
The confidence rationale explains why the finding is or is not actionable
